meaning: n.制绳者,<美>用索套捕牲口的牧人,诱人进入赌场者
examples: In order for a calf roper to become proficient he must spend a great deal of time practicing.
The purpose of the stunning is merely to enable the roper to tie the steer's legs for a score.
A Roper poll found that one in six Americans hired cleaning help last year, up from one in 10 in 1986.
Roper says mark-to-market accounting makes it possible for investors to trust what companies report about themselves.
Walsall's Ian Roper was booked for dissent but the free-kick was a brief respite.
